questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number pair from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except option (d), all follow the same logic as follows: 6*1+1*2+0*3+2*4 = 16 3*1+2*2+9*3+7*4 = 62 2*1+8*2+5*3+3*4 = 45

questions.question: If 'x' means 'addition', '-' means 'division', '/' means 'subtraction' and '+' means 'multiplication', then which of the following equation is correct?
Arrange the given words in a meaningful order. a) Written Examination b) Form Filling c) Advertisement d) Final Result e) Interview
questions.explanation: The meaningful order will be: advertisement>form filling> written examination> Interview> final result

questions.question: In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. DSBF : FZID ∷ RPDJ : ?
questions.explanation: Position of D and F, and, S and B, are interchanged. It is rearranged as→ FBSD Reverse of B→Y+1→Z Reverse of S→H+1→I Similarly, Position of R and J, and, P and D, are interchanged. It is rearranged as→ JDPR Reverse of D→W+1→X Reverse of P→K+1→L RPDJ→JXLR

questions.question: In the following question, select the related number from the given alternatives. 491 : 542 ∷ 178 : ?
questions.explanation: We perform the following operation: First digit+1; second digit - 5; third digit+1 i.e., 491 → 542 Similarly, 178 → 229

questions.question: 6 persons A, B, C, D, E and F are standing in a row facing north. C is standing to the left of A. B is standing to the right of E. F is standing to the right of A and is standing to the left of E. D is standing to the left of C. Who is standing 3rd from the right end of the row?
questions.explanation: C is standing to the left of A and B is standing to the right of E. D is standing to the left of C. And, F is standing to the right of A and is standing to the left of E. So, we get:

questions.question: How many such pairs of letters are there in the word 'NORMALIZES' each of which has as many letters between them in the word as in the English alphabet?
questions.explanation: NORMALIZES 14151813112926519 The pairs are: NO, OM, MS, AE Hence 4 such pairs.

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices can be represented first by its row and next by its column, e.g., W can be represented by 31, 42, etc., and O can be represented by 03, 34, etc. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word WORK. MATRIX-I 01234 0MWTOM 1TQWMT 2OTQMW 3MWTMO 4QTWMT MATRIX-II 56789 5RVUPV 6PKVRU 7UVRSK 8VKVRP 9RVKUR
questions.explanation: By verifying using 1st option 31 20 77 68 W O R R By verifying using 2nd option 24 11 86 99 W Q K R By verifying using 3rd option 42 34 99 78 W O R S By verifying using 4th option 12 03 95 66 W O R K So option (4) is correct.

questions.question: Choose the Venn diagram which best illustrates the three given classes in each of the following questions. Anxiety, Intelligence, Strength A. B. C. D.
questions.explanation: Anxiety, intelligence and strength are entirely different from each other.

questions.question: Q is the wife of P.R is the Brother of P.S is the daughter of Q.U is the Husband of V.R is son of U.T is Son of R. How S relates to V?
questions.explanation: Hence S is the grand-daughter of V

questions.question: There are four towns - A, B, C and D. It is observed that, B is located to the south of A and C is located to the north - east of both A and B. If D is located to the West of C then which of the following option is definitely wrong?
options: D is located to the north-west of A.
D is located to the north-east of B
D is located to the north of B.
D is located to the south-west of A.
questions.explanation: If we draw the figure based on the given information then following tree cases are possible: Hence it is clear that option (d) is definitely wrong.

questions.question: Consider the given statement/s to be true and decide which of the given conclusions/assumptions can definitely be drawn from the given statement. Statement: It would be a great achievement on the part of the government if it somehow succeeds in setting up schools in every rural area of the country to accommodate every child, and enforces attendance. Assumption I: Children in rural areas do not attend schools regularly. Assumption II: There are enough schools in urban areas to accommodate every child in these areas.
options: Both assumptions I and II are correct
Neither assumption I nor II is correct
questions.explanation: Even though the statement only talks about setting up schools in rural areas, it does not mean that the statement assumes that enough schools in urban areas are present for the accommodation of children in these areas. I follows as the statement talks about enforcing attendance in schools in rural areas, suggesting that the statement has assumed that short attendance is a problem in these areas. 1 is the right answer.
